2026-03-23 TDnet 日本特殊陶業 コスモスイニシア アイザワ証G 玉井商船 ダイコク電

snapshot: U.S. equities rebounded on March 23, with the Dow at 46,208.47 (+1.38%), the S&P 500 at 6,581.00 (+1.15%), and the Nasdaq at 21,946.76 (+1.38%). A sharp oil pullback helped trigger short covering.
Brent settled at $99.94 and WTI at $88.13. The immediate supply-shock fear eased, although Middle East risk has not disappeared.
USD/JPY moved back to 158.30 and the U.S. 10-year yield was around 4.33%. The worst of the 'bad yen' pressure near 160 has eased for now.
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 52,980 and the Chicago yen-denominated contract was 52,995, both more than 1,400 yen above the last cash close.
Reuters' expected Nikkei range is 52,500-53,000. Even with a relief opening, a gap-and-fade risk remains high after the prior washout.

drivers.driver: Oil down hard and U.S. equities up, setting up a short-cover rebound
drivers.why_today: WTI fell into the high $80s and all three major U.S. indices rose by more than 1%. That points to an initial futures-led relief move in Tokyo.

drivers.driver: USD/JPY back in the 158s eases the worst 'bad yen' pressure
drivers.why_today: USD/JPY retraced from the 159s to 158.30. That modestly eases import-cost pressure and gives domestic-facing names a little breathing room.

drivers.driver: Large-cap after-close IR and capital-policy news should dominate stock selection
drivers.why_today: Tokio Marine, 日本特殊陶業 and Cosmos Initia all released after-close disclosures that are easy to explain and likely to matter right from the opening print.

headline_inventory.headline: Reuters' New York market summary showed the Dow up 631, the S&P 500 up 1.15%, the Nasdaq up 1.38%, USD/JPY at 158.30, and the U.S. 10-year around 4.33%
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 52,980 and TOPIX futures ended at 3558.5
Reuters' March 24 outlook calls for a 52,500-53,000 Nikkei range, with a sharp rebound expected after the U.S. delayed attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure
Tokio Marine announced a strategic partnership with Berkshire's insurance group, including a 2.49% stake sale and an offsetting buyback
Nippon Sheet Glass said the privatization report is broadly factual and that its board is scheduled to decide on March 24
日本特殊陶業 revised up both its full-year net-profit outlook and dividend forecast
Cosmos Initia revised up both its FY outlook and dividend plan
Daikoku Denki raised guidance and also expanded its shareholder perk program
Aizawa Securities Group announced its year-end dividend plan, taking the annual total to 117 yen
Tamai Steamship revised up earnings guidance and approved a large ToSTNeT-3 buyback

watchlist.name: Tokio Marine Holdings
watchlist.why_today: National Indemnity, a Berkshire insurance unit, is taking 2.49%. The setup is strengthened by both the strategic tie-up and the buyback meant to offset dilution.
watchpoints: Does the market reward the buyback offset more than the dilution concern
Can the PTS strength survive the cash opening auction
Does buying stay concentrated in Tokio Marine rather than spreading thinly across insurers
falsifiers: The market focuses first on dilution from the treasury-share disposal
The tie-up is treated as strategically positive but too long-dated for immediate impact
watchlist.red_team: As a mega-cap, the stock can lose some freshness quickly after a strong opening gap.

watchlist.name: 日本特殊陶業
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY net profit from 90.0 billion yen to 116.0 billion yen and lifted the annual dividend from 186 yen to 205 yen. This is one of the cleaner large-cap number stories on the board.
watchpoints: Are both the profit revision and the dividend hike rewarded cleanly at the open
Does turnover stay strong enough for a large-cap follow-through move
Can the stock-specific revision story beat a generic external-demand rebound
falsifiers: The market reduces the revision to a simple yen-weakness effect
The session turns into pure index rebound trading and dilutes the stock-specific angle
watchlist.red_team: A meaningful part of the upgrade still leans on external conditions, especially FX.

sources.title: 特殊陶(5334) 業績予想の修正及び配当予想の修正に関するお知らせ
特殊陶の２６年３月期最終益は一転過去最高の見通し、米関税影響減り円安効果も

watchlist.name: Cosmos Initia
watchlist.why_today: It revised FY net profit from 6.5 billion yen to 7.5 billion yen and raised the annual dividend from 38 yen to 44 yen. Among real-estate names, it offers both better numbers and more shareholder return.
watchpoints: Does yield-driven re-rating buying show up right away
Can the stock outperform the real-estate group on its own disclosures
Can it absorb opening supply after a likely gap-up
falsifiers: Investors focus too much on one-off profit elements
Liquidity on the Standard market proves too thin for follow-through
watchlist.red_team: The numbers are strong, but the one-off tint has not fully disappeared.

sources.title: コスモスイニ(8844) 2026年３月期業績予想・配当予想の修正（増配）に関するお知らせ
コスモスイニ、今期経常を7％上方修正、配当も6円増額

watchlist.name: Daikoku Denki
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY ordinary-profit guidance from 7.5 billion yen to 9.1 billion yen and also expanded its shareholder perk program. Both numeric and retail-investor angles are in play.
watchpoints: Does the guidance raise matter more than the perk expansion
Can volume remain active after the opening print
Does rebound money also flow into leisure-related cyclicals
falsifiers: The move ends up being driven only by short-term perk interest
It is sold as a cyclical on any early market wobble
watchlist.red_team: The story is attractive, but the stock is still fundamentally cyclical and can move around quickly.

ダイコク電(6430) 株主優待制度の拡充に関するお知らせ

watchlist.name: Nippon Sheet Glass
watchlist.why_today: The company said the privatization report is broadly factual and that its board is scheduled to decide on March 24. This is an event-driven name that needs close monitoring rather than blind chasing.
watchpoints: Does the market continue to close the gap toward the reported 500-yen scenario
Does the company response keep event expectations alive
Can the stock avoid a simple gap-and-fade despite heavy turnover
falsifiers: The formal terms come in below what the market is assuming
The board decision is delayed or materially altered
watchlist.red_team: The timing and terms are not final yet, so event uncertainty is still very high.

sources.title: 板硝子(5202) 当社に関する報道について
板硝子はＰＴＳで急騰、非公開化の方針固めたと報道

watchlist.name: Aizawa Securities Group
watchlist.why_today: The previously undecided FY March 2026 dividend was set at 117 yen. With special-dividend elements included, yield support and rights-related demand can both matter.
watchpoints: Does dividend-capture buying start early
Can the stock outperform the broader brokerage group
Does the high-yield angle hold after the opening auction
falsifiers: The market treats the special-dividend element as non-recurring
Broader risk aversion overwhelms any yield appeal
watchlist.red_team: Because the special-dividend element is large, the market may hesitate to ascribe a durable multiple change.

sources.title: アイザワ証Ｇ(8708) 2026年３月期の期末配当（普通配当及び特別配当）予定に関するお知らせ
アイザワ証Ｇ、未定だった今期配当は21円増配

watchlist.name: Tamai Steamship
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY net profit by 2.1x and also approved a ToSTNeT-3 buyback equal to roughly 20.7% of shares outstanding. The disclosure is eye-catching even if the name is less liquid.
watchpoints: Does the scale of the buyback outweigh concern over one-off gains
Can the stock sustain a move despite thin liquidity
Does any residual shipping-theme strength spill into the name
falsifiers: The market decides the move is mostly driven by disposal gains
Shipping-theme demand fades before the stock can build follow-through
watchlist.red_team: The disclosures are flashy, but low liquidity can make the path very unstable.

deep_dive.title: Tokio Marine: Look beyond the Buffett headline and test whether the buyback-offset story sticks

lines: This is not just a generic 'Buffett-related' trade. The more important point is how the market reads the combination of treasury-share disposal and the offsetting buyback.
As a liquid insurance mega-cap, Tokio Marine can attract both index-rebound money and stock-specific capital at the same time.

After the March 23 close, the company disclosed a strategic partnership with a Berkshire insurance subsidiary and a treasury-share disposal, while also approving a buyback intended to offset dilution.
PTS trading showed a double-digit upside reaction, so the headline is still fresh going into the opening cash auction.
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than it worries about dilution from the disposal.
Does turnover remain stock-specific rather than simply spreading across the insurance sector.
Dilution concerns dominate the first read and overwhelm the buyback logic.
The partnership is treated as strategically positive but too long-dated for this year's numbers.
If the broader rebound fades after the open, a mega-cap like Tokio Marine can still face quick profit-taking.
The cooperation path may take time to become tangible, leaving the stock exposed to headline fatigue.

no_trade_conditions: If Nikkei 225 futures lose most of the 52,980 night-session rebound before the cash open and the short-cover premise breaks
If Brent or WTI re-spike sharply and wipe out the previous night's relief move
If USD/JPY snaps back toward 160 or intervention-style volatility suddenly returns
If the fresh after-close names such as Tokio Marine and 日本特殊陶業 fail to hold their opening premiums and the day turns into a pure index rebound only
content.disclaimer: This is a pre-open market digest, not investment advice. Please make your own final decisions.
